evals.description: Trigger test: activates on Terraform backend setup question
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Terraform project for our AWS infrastructure. What files should I create first and how do I set up remote state so the whole team can collaborate safely?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on drift detection question
evals.prompt: Someone on our team made manual changes in the AWS console to a security group that Terraform manages. How do I detect and fix this drift?

evals.description: Core task: writing a reusable VPC module
evals.prompt: I need to create a reusable Terraform module for a VPC with public and private subnets that I can use across dev, staging, and prod. What should the module structure look like and what variables and outputs should it expose?

evals.description: Core task: importing existing resources into state
evals.prompt: We have an S3 bucket that was created manually and I want Terraform to start managing it without destroying and recreating it. How do I do this safely?

evals.description: Core task: handling state move after refactor
evals.prompt: I refactored my Terraform config to move an aws_instance resource into a module. Now terraform plan wants to destroy and recreate the instance. How do I fix this without downtime?

evals.description: Core task: setting up multi-environment strategy
evals.prompt: We need separate dev, staging, and production environments with Terraform. Should I use workspaces or separate state files? What are the trade-offs?

evals.description: Core task: using dynamic blocks to avoid repetition
evals.prompt: I have a security group where the ingress rules vary by environment. Right now I'm copy-pasting ingress blocks. Is there a cleaner way to define these rules dynamically in Terraform?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about hardcoding AMI IDs
evals.prompt: My Terraform config has ami = 'ami-0abcdef1234567890' hardcoded. My colleague said this is bad practice. Why, and what should I do instead?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against using local state in production
evals.prompt: I've been running terraform apply from my laptop and the state file is just in my project folder. Everything works fine - do I really need to set up remote state?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about force-unlocking state without verifying
evals.prompt: Terraform is saying the state is locked and I need to apply urgently. Can I just run terraform force-unlock to clear it?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend AdministratorAccess for Terraform provider credentials
evals.prompt: What IAM permissions does Terraform need to manage our AWS infrastructure? Should I just give it AdministratorAccess to keep things simple?

evals.description: References load: points to module-patterns reference for module design question
evals.prompt: We're building a Terraform module library that multiple teams will share. I need to understand patterns like composition vs wrapper modules and how to version them. Where should I look?
